etymology.description: 氵(水, "water") + 𠯑 (phonetic) transformed to 舌(舌, "tongue") = life-giving water -> live; be alive
etymology.interpretation: The character 活 (huó, "live; be alive") symbolizes the essential role of water 氵(水) in sustaining life, enhanced by the phonetic component initially represented by 𠯑 but later transformed into 舌(舌, "tongue"). This reflects how water is crucial for vitality and life.

examples.chinese: 他活得很快乐(他活得很快樂)。
人们需要水才能活(人們需要水才能活)。
如果努力工作(如果努力工作)，我们会活得更好(我們會活得更好)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǔlì gōngzuò, wǒmen huì huó de gèng hǎo.
examples.english: If we work hard, we will live better.
